creator: wjw@digiware.nl
text:
(In reply to Kubilay Kocak from comment #1)

To start with the last one: Yes, it has passed both poudriere and portlint.
Portlint complains that the patch format could be more to its liking, but it is
the git diff format that is used in the project.

About naming...
Both version will be supported as long as they are on the release map of Ceph.
Ceph usually has 2 stable versions out, and a HEAD version.
At the moment I will track atleast the 2 stable versions.

I would prefer not continuously keep renameing 
    net/ceph -> net/ceph<version>
    new release  -> net/ceph
everytime a new majore release is done.

So I would then move net/ceph to net/ceph12.
But I have no clou as to how that is done...
Maybe you can advise?
